landing.text:
SMS, MMS & WhatsApp Messaging Service – AWS End User Messaging – Amazon Web Services
Select your cookie preferences
We use essential cookies and similar tools that are necessary to provide our site and services. We use performance cookies to collect anonymous statistics, so we can understand how customers use our site and make improvements. Essential cookies cannot be deactivated, but you can choose “Customize” or “Decline” to decline performance cookies.
If you agree, AWS and approved third parties will also use cookies to provide useful site features, remember your preferences, and display relevant content, including relevant advertising. To accept or decline all non-essential cookies, choose “Accept” or “Decline.” To make more detailed choices, choose “Customize.”
Accept
Decline
Customize
Customize cookie preferences
We use cookies and similar tools (collectively, "cookies") for the following purposes.
Essential
Essential cookies are necessary to provide our site and services and cannot be deactivated. They are usually set in response to your actions on the site, such as setting your privacy preferences, signing in, or filling in forms.
Allowed
Performance
Performance cookies provide anonymous statistics about how customers navigate our site so we can improve site experience and performance. Approved third parties may perform analytics on our behalf, but they cannot use the data for their own purposes.
Allowed
Functional
Functional cookies help us provide useful site features, remember your preferences, and display relevant content. Approved third parties may set these cookies to provide certain site features. If you do not allow these cookies, then some or all of these services may not function properly.
Allowed
Advertising
Advertising cookies may be set through our site by us or our advertising partners and help us deliver relevant marketing content. If you do not allow these cookies, you will experience less relevant advertising.
Allowed
Blocking some types of cookies may impact your experience of our sites. You may review and change your choices at any time by selecting Cookie preferences in the footer of this site. We and selected third-parties use cookies or similar technologies as specified in the
AWS Cookie Notice
.
Cancel
Save preferences
Your privacy choices
We and our advertising partners (“we”) may use information we collect from or about you to show you ads on other websites and online services. Under certain laws, this activity is referred to as “cross-context behavioral advertising” or “targeted advertising.”
To opt out of our use of cookies or similar technologies to engage in these activities, select “Opt out of cross-context behavioral ads” and “Save preferences” below. If you clear your browser cookies or visit this site from a different device or browser, you will need to make your selection again. For more information about cookies and how we use them, read our
Cookie Notice
.
Allow cross-context behavioral ads
Opt out of cross-context behavioral ads
To opt out of the use of other identifiers, such as contact information, for these activities, fill out the form
here
.
For more information about how AWS handles your information, read the
AWS Privacy Notice
.
Cancel
Save preferences
Unable to save cookie preferences
We will only store essential cookies at this time, because we were unable to save your cookie preferences.
If you want to change your cookie preferences, try again later using the link in the AWS console footer, or contact support if the problem persists.

Products
Business Applications
AWS End User Messaging
SMS, RCS, MMS, and WhatsApp Conversations, built on AWS
Messaging channels that plug directly into your infrastructure for a better builder experience and faster time to value
Get Started
What is AWS End User Messaging?
AWS End User Messaging empowers developers to integrate scalable and reliable messaging capabilities into their applications. Whether it's time-sensitive alerts, one-time passwords, or two-way communications, AWS End User Messaging helps ensure your messages reach their local or global destination across multiple channels like SMS, MMS, WhatsApp, push, and text to voice. AWS End User Messaging helps unlock the full potential of your messaging strategy by connecting your applications directly to your end-users across the channels they use and trust.
Conversational Experiences
Build AI-powered conversations where customers interact naturally with your business through SMS, RCS, or WhatsApp. Connect inbound messages to your Lambda functions and Bedrock for intelligent responses, creating complete application interfaces right in messaging apps customers already use. With RCS and WhatsApp, your AI agents assist customers and complete transactions under your verified brand identity — so customers know they're engaging with your business.
Build conversational AI
Promotions
Reach your audience instantly with product launches, limited-time offers, and event invitations through SMS, MMS, RCS, and WhatsApp — with rich media content that drives engagement. Verified brand identity on RCS and WhatsApp helps ensure your promotional messages are recognized as legitimate, reducing spam concerns.
Explore promotional channels
Notifications
Keep customers informed with order confirmations, shipping updates, appointment reminders, and account alerts. For one-time passwords, Notify gets you sending in minutes — no phone number registration, no carrier approval, just call the API. Built-in fraud protection stops anomalous traffic to prevent unexpected costs, and pre-approved templates mean you can safely deploy two-factor authentication without weeks of setup.
Get started with Notify
Benefits of AWS End User Messaging
Send Messages at Scale
AWS End User Messaging provides the global scale, resiliency, and flexibility required to deliver SMS, RCS, WhatsApp, push, and voice messaging capabilities in your web, mobile, and business applications. Trust that your messages are delivered to the geographies that matter most to your business.
Reduce Spam Concerns with Verified Identity
Build confidence with end users through multiple layers of protection. RCS and WhatsApp provide verified brand identities — your logo, name, and verified badge where the verified checkmark next to your brand name helps reduce customers suspecting your valid messages are spam or phishing. SMS Protect validates phone numbers and detects fraud before you send, identifying anomalous traffic to prevent unexpected costs.
Get Started Fast, Scale on Your Terms
Notify lets you start sending OTP messages in minutes — no phone number registration required, no carrier approval, just call the API and send. When you're ready to grow and need your own phone number or brand identity, our AI-powered registration reviewer gives you faster feedback on your submissions so you can get through the registration process sooner. Start fast with Notify, then scale to dedicated numbers, verified brands, and full messaging capabilities on your terms.
Messaging Channels in AWS
AWS End User Messaging brings messaging channels into the AWS ecosystem where your core applications live. Inbound messages flow to SNS, EventBridge, or Kinesis, enabling seamless connections to your applications and services. Your messaging channels and applications live in the same place — in AWS — creating a better builder experience with tightly coupled, real-time interactions, less complexity, and faster time to value.
Show more

RCS

Phone Number Validate
RCS
Send verified, branded messages with pay-as-you-go pricing based on destination country. RCS messages are charged only upon delivery, with pricing that varies by message type and country.
United States RCS Messaging
In the US, RCS messages are classified as Rich or Rich Media based on content. Rich messages are billed per 160-byte (UTF-8) segment. Rich Media messages are billed at a flat rate per message. Each message has two cost components: a transport fee and a carrier fee. Businesses are only charged when a message is successfully delivered.
Meter
AWS fee
Carrier fee
Rich RCS (outbound)
$0.00700 per segment
$0.00494 per segment
Rich Media RCS (outbound)
$0.02000 per message
$0.00949 per message
Inbound Rich RCS
$0.00700 per segment
$0.00218 per segment
Inbound Rich Media RCS
$0.02000 per message
$0.00737 per message
Rich RCS includes:
• Text (billed per 160-character segment)
• Suggested replies
• Suggested action: Dial a phone number• Suggested action: Open URL (full browser)
Rich Media RCS includes:
• Rich cards or carousels
• Media file attachments (images, video, audio)
• Suggested action: Open URL in a webview
• Suggested action: View location or Share location
• Suggested action: Create calendar event
Message classification is automatic based on content. Suggested replies, Dial, and Open URL can be added to any message type without changing its classification. Any media, rich card, carousel, or advanced action upgrades the message to Rich Media.
International RCS Messaging
Outside the US, RCS messages are billed per message or per conversation. Messages up to 160 characters are billed as RCS Basic. Messages over 160 characters, or containing rich cards, file attachments, carousels, or any suggestion added to any message type are billed as RCS Single. Businesses are only charged when a message is successfully delivered.

All prices are in USD. Outbound and inbound prices are the same for international RCS messaging.
RCS Conversation Pricing
Conversational pricing requires:
• The RCS agent must be registered as a Conversational agent at the time of creation
• The business must both send and receive a message to/from its end user within a 24-hour window
• Once both conditions are met, all subsequent messages within the 24-hour window are included in the conversational rate
If either condition is not met, messages are billed individually as Basic or Single. For full conversation session rules, refer to the AWS End User Messaging RCS developer documentation.
RCS pricing per country (CSV)
SMS
Send text messages globally with pay-as-you-go pricing based on destination country, protect SMS from artificially inflated traffic, and deliver multimedia experiences in the United States and Canada.
SMS
SMS Protect
MMS
SMS
SMS
The price for sending SMS messages varies between countries and regions, and in some cases, between carriers in the same country or region. These prices change regularly.
Carrier fees will vary based on the type of number sending the message. See
FAQs
for more information on carrier fees.
To qualify for the local route price, you must comply with India specific regulations. The default route to India is via International Long Distance Operator (ILDO) routes. For more information please refer to
AWS End User Messaging
.
For international sends (e.g., sending messages from a different country phone number than the destination country number), the cost per message will be charged based on the destination country price.
Messages sent to Canada via a toll-free number will be charged $0.00581 per message fee and $0.00705 carrier fee.
Use the following pricing tool to see the prices for sending and receiving messages for each carrier in a country or region.  You can download outbound SMS prices per country via CSV below.

SMS Protect
General information:
SMS Protect fees are incremental to standard SMSmessage charges
Messages using "allow" or "block" configurations have no additional charges
Messages using "monitor" or "filter" configurations incur an additional per message charge for Protect
SMS Protect is available in all countries where AWS End User Messaging supports SMS

WhatsApp
WhatsApp
AWS charges two fees for each WhatsApp message:
1.  AWS message fee: $0.005 per outbound message globally (except India Authentication and Utility messages at $0.002), and $0.001 per inbound message globally.
2. Meta message fee: price varies for each country and message type.
The Meta message fee is set by Meta and subject to change by Meta. We will make reasonable efforts to notify you of increases to the Meta message fee.
We implement Meta's volume-tiered pricing for Meta message fee. The volume-tiered prices, tier thresholds, and discounts are set by Meta, and subject to change by Meta. Volume tiers on ongoing monthly usage are determined solely by Meta. AWS applies the volume tiers to ongoing usage as and when Meta informs us of tier changes. For more information about tier thresholds and current rates, see
Meta's WhatsApp Business Platform pricing documentation
.
AWS charges the Meta message fee using the rates in Meta's INR rate card, including for volume-tiered pricing. AWS will charge the Meta message fee in USD by applying the INR to USD exchange rate as of 4/1/2026. On the first day of each quarter, AWS may update the USD price it charges for the Meta message fee to reflect changes to the INR to USD exchange rate.
Message types are:
Marketing
— Enables you to achieve a wide range of goals, from generating awareness to driving sales and retargeting customers. Examples include new product, service, or feature announcements, targeted promotions/offers, and cart abandonment reminders.
Utility
— Enables you to follow-up on user actions or requests. Examples include opt-in confirmation, order/delivery management (e.g., delivery update), account updates, alerts (payment reminder), and feedback surveys.
Authentication
—  Enables you authenticate users with one-time passcodes, potentially at multiple steps in the login process (e.g., account verification, account recovery, integrity challenges). Authentication also includes the Authentication-International message type that applies to countries with international sending rates. See
https://developers.facebook.com/docs/whatsapp/pricing/authentication-international-rates
to see if these international rates apply to your business.
Service
— Enables you to resolve customer inquiries.
Meta message fee at list price and at volume tiers, effective April 1, 2026 are below.
AWS WhatsApp Meta Fee CSV
AWS WhatsApp Meta Fee Volume Tiers CSV
Push Notifications
Push notifications
You pay $0 for the first 1,000,000 push notifications that you send each month, and $0.000001 for each push notification that you send after that.
Voice
Voice (outbound only)
The price for sending voice messages varies between countries, and occasionally between carriers within the same country or region. These prices are subject to change.
To send voice messages using AWS End User Messaging, you must obtain at least one dedicated, voice-enabled long code. You can lease local phone numbers in a variety of countries directly through the AWS End User Messaging console. A long code is a standard telephone number that your message is sent from.
Note: Pricing differs for voice-enabled long codes vs. SMS-enabled long codes. You can’t use voice-enabled long codes to send SMS messages unless the phone number also has SMS enabled. SMS-enabled long codes are not available in all countries, and are often more expensive than voice-enabled long codes.

What is AWS End User Messaging SMS? - AWS End User Messaging SMS
Documentation
AWS End User Messaging SMS
User Guide
Are you a first-time AWS End User Messaging SMS user?
Features of AWS End User Messaging SMS
Accessing AWS End User Messaging SMS
Regional availability
Note
The SMS, MMS, and voice (text to speech) features of Amazon Pinpoint are now called AWS End User
   Messaging.
AWS End User Messaging SMS
is an application-to-person (A2P) SMS, MMS, RCS, and voice messaging service which
  provides the global scale, resiliency, and flexibility required to deliver messaging in any
  web, mobile, or business applications. SMS messages are used for their most important and urgent
  communications as SMS proves to be the most effective and ubiquitous communication channel
  available. Customers prioritize time critical and must-deliver use-cases such as one-time password
  (OTP) login and authentication, marketing messages, citizen outreach, delivery status updates, or
  appointment reminders to name a few.
Multimedia messaging service (MMS) is an extension of SMS that provides the ability to send media
  messages to a mobile phone which includes image, audio, text, or video files. You can use MMS to
  improve engagement through a variety of branding, workflow, and marketing use cases.
Rich Communication Services (RCS) for business provides verified brand identity, delivery receipts, and
  improved messaging performance for your messages. AWS End User Messaging SMS supports sending text messages via RCS with
  automatic SMS fallback when RCS is unavailable. You create an AWS RCS Agent to represent your
  brand, and use phone pools to enable seamless RCS-to-SMS fallback. For more information, see
RCS messaging
.
The information in this user guide is intended for all AWS End User Messaging SMS users, including
  marketers, business users, and developers. This guide contains information that's especially
  helpful for users who mainly interact with AWS End User Messaging SMS by using the AWS Management Console.
Note
AWS End User Messaging SMS uses the
pinpoint-sms-voice-v2
API namespace.
There are several other documen

What is AWS End User Messaging SMS? - AWS End User Messaging SMS
Select your cookie preferences
We use essential cookies and similar tools that are necessary to provide our site and services. We use performance cookies to collect anonymous statistics, so we can understand how customers use our site and make improvements. Essential cookies cannot be deactivated, but you can choose “Customize” or “Decline” to decline performance cookies.
If you agree, AWS and approved third parties will also use cookies to provide useful site features, remember your preferences, and display relevant content, including relevant advertising. To accept or decline all non-essential cookies, choose “Accept” or “Decline.” To make more detailed choices, choose “Customize.”
Accept
Decline
Customize
Customize cookie preferences
We use cookies and similar tools (collectively, "cookies") for the following purposes.
Essential
Essential cookies are necessary to provide our site and services and cannot be deactivated. They are usually set in response to your actions on the site, such as setting your privacy preferences, signing in, or filling in forms.
Performance
Performance cookies provide anonymous statistics about how customers navigate our site so we can improve site experience and performance. Approved third parties may perform analytics on our behalf, but they cannot use the data for their own purposes.
Allowed
Functional
Functional cookies help us provide useful site features, remember your preferences, and display relevant content. Approved third parties may set these cookies to provide certain site features. If you do not allow these cookies, then some or all of these services may not function properly.
Allowed
Advertising
Advertising cookies may be set through our site by us or our advertising partners and help us deliver relevant marketing content. If you do not allow these cookies, you will experience less relevant advertising.
Allowed
Blocking some types of cookies may impact your experience of our sites. You may review and change your choices at any time by selecting Cookie preferences in the footer of this site. We and selected third-parties use cookies or similar technologies as specified in the
AWS Cookie Notice
.
Cancel
Save preferences
Unable to save cookie preferences
We will only store essential cookies at this time, because we were unable to save your cookie preferences.
If you want to change your cookie preferences, try again later using the link in the AWS console footer, or contact support if the problem persists.

Regional availability
Note
The SMS, MMS, and voice (text to speech) features of Amazon Pinpoint are now called AWS End User
   Messaging.
AWS End User Messaging SMS
is an application-to-person (A2P) SMS, MMS, RCS, and voice messaging service which
  provides the global scale, resiliency, and flexibility required to deliver messaging in any
  web, mobile, or business applications. SMS messages are used for their most important and urgent
  communications as SMS proves to be the most effective and ubiquitous communication channel
  available. Customers prioritize time critical and must-deliver use-cases such as one-time password
  (OTP) login and authentication, marketing messages, citizen outreach, delivery status updates, or
  appointment reminders to name a few.
Multimedia messaging service (MMS) is an extension of SMS that provides the ability to send media
  messages to a mobile phone which includes image, audio, text, or video files. You can use MMS to
  improve engagement through a variety of branding, workflow, and marketing use cases.
Rich Communication Services (RCS) for business provides verified brand identity, delivery receipts, and
  improved messaging performance for your messages. AWS End User Messaging SMS supports sending text messages via RCS with
  automatic SMS fallback when RCS is unavailable. You create an AWS RCS Agent to represent your

RCS messaging
.
The information in this user guide is intended for all AWS End User Messaging SMS users, including

analysis.name: AWS End User Messaging (Pinpoint SMS)
analysis.tagline: SMS, RCS, MMS, and WhatsApp Conversations, built on AWS
analysis.value_proposition: Scalable, reliable messaging channels (SMS, MMS, RCS, WhatsApp, push, voice) that plug directly into AWS infrastructure for developers.
analysis.ideal_customer: AWS-native developers and enterprises building transactional, promotional, and conversational messaging into their applications

differentiators: Native AWS infrastructure integration (Lambda, Bedrock, CloudWatch)
Notify API for OTP without number registration or carrier approval
Built-in fraud protection to prevent runaway costs
Verified brand identity on RCS and WhatsApp
Pay-as-you-go pricing charged only on successful delivery

recent_ships.what: RCS messaging with rich cards, carousels, suggestions across 12+ countries
recent_ships.source: docs: Launching RCS in Austria, Brazil, Canada, France, Germany, Mexico, Netherlands, Peru, Singapore, Spain, UK, US

recent_ships.what: Notify API for simplified OTP delivery
recent_ships.source: landing: Notify gets you sending in minutes — no phone number registration

free_tier.limits: SMS/MMS and Voice sandbox for testing with simulator phone numbers

tiers.name: Rich RCS (US outbound)
tiers.headline_inclusions: $0.00700 AWS fee + $0.00494 carrier fee per 160-byte segment

tiers.name: Rich Media RCS (US outbound)
tiers.headline_inclusions: $0.02000 AWS fee + $0.00949 carrier fee per message

tiers.name: Inbound Rich RCS (US)
tiers.headline_inclusions: $0.00700 AWS fee + $0.00218 carrier fee per segment

tiers.name: Inbound Rich Media RCS (US)
tiers.headline_inclusions: $0.02000 AWS fee + $0.00737 carrier fee per message

tiers.name: RCS Basic (International)
tiers.headline_inclusions: Per-message pricing varies by country (e.g. AT $0.0652, BR $0.0300)

tiers.name: RCS Single (International)
tiers.headline_inclusions: Per-message pricing for >160 char or rich content (e.g. AT $0.0677, BR $0.0463)

pricing.notes: Pure usage-based pricing across RCS, SMS, WhatsApp, Push, Voice, Notify, phone numbers, and validation. Charged only on successful delivery. Prices vary by destination country and message type. Two-component pricing (AWS fee + carrier fee) for US RCS. No seat-based tiers; no free monthly allotment beyond sandbox. G2 reviews page returned no content.
